回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	114學年度第2學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 英語語言學概論(一) (Introduction to English Linguistics I)	開課班級 : 應英一年級	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 李盈興	必選修 : 必修	 	 


1.教學目標
This course is designed for students who are no linguists yet, but who will need some familiarity with the fundamentals of linguistic knowledge in order to help them practice their profession in the future. This includes specialties in such fields as anthropology, composition, education, English as a second language, experimental phonetics, folklore, foreign languages, literature, psychology, speech-language pathology, reading, and technical and business communication. Since this is an introductory course, students are oriented to “do” linguistics or “analyze” languages with a focus on English data, supplemented with Mandarin data. Student will thus review what they have already known or acquire the new patterns of both languages from a linguistic perspective. The course is organized as follows. The first half of the course covers the theoretic areas of pragmatics, semantics, syntax, morphology, and phonology, respectively. Once students have a basic understanding of these areas, they will have little trouble mastering the applied areas that overlap with their own field of specialization. The second half convers the applied areas that are the most important to the greatest number of neighboring fields: language processing, language variation, language change, first-language acquisition, second-language acquisition, written language, and the neurology of language. 


2.教學綱要
The content of this semester contains:
- Pragmatics: the use of language in context
- Semantics: the meanings of words, phrases, and sentences
- Syntax: the structure of phrases, clauses, and sentences
- Morphology: the formation of words
- Phonology: the psychological system of speech sounds
- Phonetics: the physical dimensions of speech sounds

anguage and Linguistics (13th Edition).

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Evaluations: (1) Midterm Exam 30%, (2) Final Exam 30%, (3) Reviews 20%, (4) Presentation 10%, (5) Participation 10%. 
Requirements: Students are required to prepare the weekly materials before they come to class. Students should participate in the midterm exam, final exam, group presentation, and two in-class quizzes. The teacher will check the attendance; those who are absent or come late will lose 1 score from the final grades for each time. Students should also show respect to others in the class by not talking aloud, not wandering from the seats, and not surfing the Internet on their smartphones. They should understand and be responsible for their grades. 
 


8.永續發展目標(SDGs)：SDG4 優質教育


9.大學社會責任(USR)關聯性：高
